text: I am enveloped by an air of careful curiosity here. In the first moment, I see myself as an observer outside a glass wall, glimpsing people at some deliberate activity, perhaps recording or interviewing—signs of inquiry and the pursuit of knowledge. Stepping inside, I’m greeted by a celebration of research and development—a space alive with the visual language of innovation, accompanied by contemplative texts about technology, meaning, and togetherness. I feel simultaneously small and significant, reminded that even in distant corners of progress, a simple act of witnessing or questioning binds us all together in our shared search for understanding.
